label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at the question. It's asking what the Indians believed according to the letter. The letter is about the Six Nations refusing an offer to send their boys to William and Mary College. They say that even though the offer is kind, they don't want to accept it. The reason they give is that when their young people went to colleges in the northern provinces, they came back with bad manners and couldn't live in the woods or do anything useful. The Six Nations believe that the education system in the colleges is not suitable for their way of life. They also offer to educate the sons of the Virginians if they send them, implying that their way of education is better for making "men" out of the young. Now, looking at the options: A. it would be better for their boys to receive some schooling - No, they are refusing the offer, so this is not what they believe. B. they were being insulted by the offer - The letter says they are not offended and thank the officials, so this is incorrect. C. they knew more about science than the officials - The letter mentions they were taught all your sciences, but it's not said they know more about it. D. they had better way of educating young men - This is supported by the letter. They are
